tool.cpp(3) Library Functions Manual tool.cpp(3)

NAME

tool.cpp

SYNOPSIS

Functions


std::mutex & outputRegistryMutex ()
std::unordered_set< std::string > & outputRegistry ()
bool registerOutputPath (const std::string &path)
void unregisterOutputPath (const std::string &path)
int main (int argc, const char **argv)

Function Documentation

int main (int argc, const char ** argv)

Definition at line 813 of file defer/tool.cpp.

813                                       {
814   InitLLVM InitLLVM(argc, argv);
815 
816   // Capture CWD before anything else can change it
817   g_originalCwd = fs::current_path();
818 
819   // Hide all the LLVM internal options that aren't relevant to our tool
820   cl::HideUnrelatedOptions(ToolCategory);
821 
822   cl::ParseCommandLineOptions(argc, argv, "ascii-defer transformation tool\n");
823 
824   fs::path outputDir = fs::path(OutputDirectoryOption.getValue());
825   // Make output directory absolute relative to original CWD
826   if (!outputDir.is_absolute()) {
827     outputDir = g_originalCwd / outputDir;
828   }
829   fs::path inputRoot;
830   if (!InputRootOption.getValue().empty()) {
831     inputRoot = fs::path(InputRootOption.getValue());
832   } else {
833     inputRoot = fs::current_path();
834   }
835 
836   // Make input root absolute for reliable path computation
837   if (!inputRoot.is_absolute()) {
838     std::error_code ec;
839     inputRoot = fs::absolute(inputRoot, ec);
840     if (ec) {
841       llvm::errs() << "Failed to resolve input root path: " << ec.message() << "\n";
842       return 1;
843     }
844   }
845 
846   std::vector<std::string> sourcePaths;
847   for (const auto &path : SourcePaths) {
848     if (!path.empty()) {
849       sourcePaths.push_back(path);
850     }
851   }
852 
853   if (sourcePaths.empty()) {
854     llvm::errs() << "No translation units specified for transformation. Provide positional source paths.\n";
855     return 1;
856   }
857 
858   if (fs::exists(outputDir)) {
859     if (!fs::is_directory(outputDir)) {
860       llvm::errs() << "Output path exists and is not a directory: " << outputDir.c_str() << "\n";
861       return 1;
862     }
863   } else {
864     std::error_code errorCode;
865     fs::create_directories(outputDir, errorCode);
866     if (errorCode) {
867       llvm::errs() << "Failed to create output directory: " << outputDir.c_str() << " - " << errorCode.message()
868                    << "\n";
869       return 1;
870     }
871   }
872 
873   // Load compilation database
874   std::string buildPath = BuildPath.getValue();
875   if (buildPath.empty()) {
876     buildPath = ".";
877   }
878   std::string errorMessage;
879   std::unique_ptr<tooling::CompilationDatabase> compilations =
880       tooling::CompilationDatabase::loadFromDirectory(buildPath, errorMessage);
881   if (!compilations) {
882     llvm::errs() << "Error loading compilation database from '" << buildPath << "': " << errorMessage << "\n";
883     return 1;
884   }
885 
886   tooling::ClangTool tool(*compilations, sourcePaths);
887 
888   // Build the list of arguments to prepend for system header resolution
889   // LibTooling uses CC1 mode internally which has different include path handling than
890   // the clang driver. We use -Xclang to pass CC1-level flags that properly configure
891   // system include paths for LibTooling's CompilerInvocation.
892   std::vector<std::string> prependArgs;
893 
894   // Try to find clang resource directory at runtime
895   // Priority: 1) CLANG_RESOURCE_DIR compile-time path, 2) Runtime detection via common paths
896   std::string resourceDir;
897 
898 #ifdef CLANG_RESOURCE_DIR
899   if (llvm::sys::fs::exists(CLANG_RESOURCE_DIR)) {
900     resourceDir = CLANG_RESOURCE_DIR;
901     // llvm::errs() << "Using embedded clang resource directory: " << resourceDir << "\n";
902   } else {
903     if (!QuietMode) {
904       llvm::errs() << "Embedded clang resource directory not found: " << CLANG_RESOURCE_DIR << "\n";
905     }
906   }
907 #endif
908 
909   // Runtime detection if embedded path doesn't work
910   if (resourceDir.empty()) {
911     // Common locations for clang resource directories
912     std::vector<std::string> searchPaths;
913 
914 #ifdef __APPLE__
915     // Homebrew LLVM on Apple Silicon
916     searchPaths.push_back("/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/lib/clang");
917     // Homebrew LLVM on Intel Mac
918     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/local/opt/llvm/lib/clang");
919     // Xcode's clang
920     searchPaths.push_back(
921         "/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Toolchains/XcodeDefault.xctoolchain/usr/lib/clang");
922     // CommandLineTools clang
923     searchPaths.push_back("/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/lib/clang");
924 #endif
925 #ifdef __linux__
926     // System LLVM installations
927     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/lib/llvm-22/lib/clang");
928     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/lib/llvm-21/lib/clang");
929     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/lib/llvm-20/lib/clang");
930     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/lib/clang");
931     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/local/lib/clang");
932 #endif
933     // Universal fallback
934     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/local/lib/clang");
935 
936     for (const auto &basePath : searchPaths) {
937       if (!llvm::sys::fs::exists(basePath)) {
938         continue;
939       }
940 
941       // Find the highest version subdirectory
942       std::error_code ec;
943       std::string bestVersion;
944       int bestMajor = 0;
945 
946       for (llvm::sys::fs::directory_iterator dir(basePath, ec), dirEnd; !ec && dir != dirEnd; dir.increment(ec)) {
947         std::string name = llvm::sys::path::filename(dir->path()).str();
948         // Parse version number (e.g., "22", "21.1.0", "21")
949         int major = 0;
950         if (sscanf(name.c_str(), "%d", &major) == 1 && major > bestMajor) {
951           bestMajor = major;
952           bestVersion = dir->path();
953         }
954       }
955 
956       if (!bestVersion.empty()) {
957         resourceDir = bestVersion;
958         if (!QuietMode) {
959           llvm::errs() << "Found clang resource directory at runtime: " << resourceDir << "\n";
960         }
961         break;
962       }
963     }
964 
965     if (resourceDir.empty() && !QuietMode) {
966       llvm::errs() << "Warning: Could not find clang resource directory\n";
967     }
968   }
969 
970   // Disable default system includes - we'll add them back explicitly in the correct order
971   // This prevents clang's builtin path from shadowing SDK system headers
972   prependArgs.push_back("-nostdinc");
973 
974   if (!resourceDir.empty()) {
975     prependArgs.push_back(std::string("-resource-dir=") + resourceDir);
976   }
977 
978   // Add target triple - LibTooling needs this to validate architecture-specific flags
979   // Without a target, flags like -mavx2 cause "unsupported option for target ''" errors
980 #ifdef __APPLE__
981 #ifdef __arm64__
982   prependArgs.push_back("-target");
983   prependArgs.push_back("arm64-apple-darwin");
984   // llvm::errs() << "Using target: arm64-apple-darwin\n";
985 #else
986   prependArgs.push_back("-target");
987   prependArgs.push_back("x86_64-apple-darwin");
988   // llvm::errs() << "Using target: x86_64-apple-darwin\n";
989 #endif
990 #elif defined(__linux__)
991 #ifdef __aarch64__
992   prependArgs.push_back("-target");
993   prependArgs.push_back("aarch64-linux-gnu");
994   // llvm::errs() << "Using target: aarch64-linux-gnu\n";
995 #else
996   prependArgs.push_back("-target");
997   prependArgs.push_back("x86_64-linux-gnu");
998   // llvm::errs() << "Using target: x86_64-linux-gnu\n";
999 #endif
1000 #endif
1001 
1002   // Override the sysroot for macOS. Homebrew's LLVM config file sets -isysroot
1003   // to CommandLineTools SDK, but we strip that from compile_commands.json and
1004   // set our own explicitly to ensure consistent behavior.
1005   std::vector<std::string> appendArgs;
1006   std::string selectedSDK;
1007 #ifdef __APPLE__
1008   {
1009     const char *sdkPaths[] = {
1010         // Xcode SDK (preferred - most complete headers and frameworks)
1011         "/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/"
1012         "MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk",
1013         // CommandLineTools SDK (fallback for users without Xcode)
1014         "/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk",
1015     };
1016 
1017     for (const char *sdk : sdkPaths) {
1018       if (llvm::sys::fs::exists(sdk)) {
1019         selectedSDK = sdk;
1020         break;
1021       }
1022     }
1023 
1024     if (!selectedSDK.empty()) {
1025       prependArgs.push_back("-isysroot");
1026       prependArgs.push_back(selectedSDK);
1027       // llvm::errs() << "Using macOS SDK: " << selectedSDK << "\n";
1028 
1029       const std::string sdkUsrInclude = selectedSDK + "/usr/include";
1030       if (llvm::sys::fs::exists(sdkUsrInclude)) {
1031         appendArgs.push_back("-isystem");
1032         appendArgs.push_back(sdkUsrInclude);
1033       }
1034 
1035       // Ensure ptrcheck.h is discoverable for SDK headers that probe it.
1036       // Prefer CLT clang 17 include path, then Xcode toolchain fallback.
1037       const char *ptrcheckIncludeCandidates[] = {
1038           "/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/lib/clang/17/include",
1039           "/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Toolchains/XcodeDefault.xctoolchain/usr/lib/clang/17/include",
1040       };
1041       for (const char *candidate : ptrcheckIncludeCandidates) {
1042         if (llvm::sys::fs::exists(candidate)) {
1043           appendArgs.push_back("-isystem");
1044           appendArgs.push_back(candidate);
1045           break;
1046         }
1047       }
1048     } else {
1049       llvm::errs() << "Warning: No macOS SDK found, system headers may not be available\n";
1050     }
1051   }
1052 #endif
1053 
1054   // Build list of system include paths to add as -isystem paths.
1055   // LibTooling (cc1 mode) doesn't automatically add system include paths like
1056   // the clang driver does, so we add them explicitly:
1057   // We use -isysroot to let cc1 mode find SDK headers, and DON'T add clang's builtin
1058   // include path explicitly since cc1 mode searches it automatically and it would
1059   // shadow the system headers when included with -isystem.
1060   // Note: We skip adding resourceDir/include as -isystem because cc1 mode
1061   // searches the clang resource directory automatically BEFORE -isystem paths,
1062   // which would cause it to look for stdio.h in the wrong place.
1063 
1064   // NOTE: We rely on -isysroot (set in prependArgs) to find system headers.
1065   // LibTooling on LLVM 21 has a bug where __has_include() evaluates even in non-taken
1066   // preprocessor branches and creates VFS entries for non-existent files (ptrcheck.h).
1067   // By using -isysroot alone without explicitly adding -isystem paths, we avoid
1068   // triggering this bug while still allowing cc1 mode to find system headers.
1069 
1070   // Single consolidated argument adjuster that:
1071   // 1. Preserves compiler path (first arg)
1072   // 2. Inserts prepend args immediately after compiler (-nostdlibinc, -resource-dir, -isysroot)
1073   // 3. Strips unnecessary flags from remaining args
1074   // 4. Adds system include paths as -isystem at the end (after project -I paths)
1075   // 5. Adds defer tool parsing define BEFORE the "--" separator
1076   std::string inputRootStr = inputRoot.string();
1077   auto consolidatedAdjuster = [prependArgs, appendArgs, inputRootStr](const tooling::CommandLineArguments &args,
1078                                                                       StringRef) {
1079     // Helper to check if a path is under the project root
1080     auto isProjectPath = [&inputRootStr](const std::string &path) -> bool {
1081       if (inputRootStr.empty())
1082         return false;
1083       // Normalize both paths for comparison
1084       std::error_code ec;
1085       auto normalizedPath = fs::canonical(path, ec);
1086       if (ec)
1087         return false; // Path doesn't exist or can't be resolved
1088       auto normalizedRoot = fs::canonical(inputRootStr, ec);
1089       if (ec)
1090         return false;
1091       // Check if the path starts with the root
1092       std::string pathStr = normalizedPath.string();
1093       std::string rootStr = normalizedRoot.string();
1094       if (pathStr.find(rootStr) != 0)
1095         return false;
1096       // Exclude .deps-cache directory - these are cached dependencies that use
1097       // angled includes (<header.h>) and need -isystem, not -iquote
1098       if (pathStr.find("/.deps-cache/") != std::string::npos)
1099         return false;
1100       return true;
1101     };
1102     auto isPublicIncludePath = [&inputRootStr](const std::string &path) -> bool {
1103       if (inputRootStr.empty())
1104         return false;
1105       std::error_code ec;
1106       auto normalizedPath = fs::canonical(path, ec);
1107       if (ec)
1108         return false;
1109       auto normalizedRoot = fs::canonical(inputRootStr, ec);
1110       if (ec)
1111         return false;
1112       std::string pathStr = normalizedPath.string();
1113       std::string rootStr = normalizedRoot.string();
1114       std::string publicInclude = rootStr + "/include";
1115       return pathStr == publicInclude || pathStr.find(publicInclude + "/") == 0;
1116     };
1117     tooling::CommandLineArguments result;
1118 
1119     if (args.empty()) {
1120       return result;
1121     }
1122 
1123     // First: preserve the compiler path (first argument)
1124     result.push_back(args[0]);
1125 
1126     // Second: add the prepend args right after the compiler
1127     for (const auto &arg : prependArgs) {
1128       result.push_back(arg);
1129     }
1130 
1131     // Third: process remaining arguments, stripping unnecessary flags
1132     // IMPORTANT: Convert -I to -iquote for project include paths.
1133     // Clang include search order for <header.h>:  -I paths -> -isystem paths -> system paths
1134     // Clang include search order for "header.h":  -iquote paths -> -I paths -> -isystem paths -> system paths
1135     //
1136     // The problem: Project -I paths are searched BEFORE -isystem paths for <stdio.h>.
1137     // This causes LibTooling to look for <stdio.h> in the project's lib/ directory first.
1138     //
1139     // The fix: Convert project -I to -iquote. Then <stdio.h> skips project paths entirely
1140     // and finds system headers in -isystem paths instead.
1141     //
1142     // ALSO: Collect all -isystem paths and reorder them so clang builtins come FIRST.
1143     // LibTooling on LLVM 21 has a VFS bug where it creates phantom entries for headers
1144     // in -isystem directories even when the header doesn't exist there.
1145     std::vector<std::string> collectedIsystemPaths;
1146     bool foundSeparator = false;
1147     size_t separatorIndex = 0;
1148     for (size_t i = 1; i < args.size(); ++i) {
1149       const std::string &arg = args[i];
1150 
1151       // When we hit "--", note its position and break
1152       if (arg == "--") {
1153         foundSeparator = true;
1154         separatorIndex = i;
1155         break;
1156       }
1157 
1158       // Skip sanitizer flags
1159       if (arg.find("-fsanitize") != std::string::npos)
1160         continue;
1161       if (arg.find("-fno-sanitize") != std::string::npos)
1162         continue;
1163       // Skip debug info flags (not needed for AST parsing)
1164       if (arg == "-g" || arg == "-g2" || arg == "-g3")
1165         continue;
1166       if (arg == "-fno-eliminate-unused-debug-types")
1167         continue;
1168       if (arg == "-fno-inline")
1169         continue;
1170       // Strip -resource-dir flags and their arguments - we added our embedded path
1171       if (arg == "-resource-dir") {
1172         ++i;
1173         continue;
1174       }
1175       if (arg.find("-resource-dir=") == 0)
1176         continue;
1177       // Strip -isysroot flags and their arguments - we added our embedded SDK path
1178       if (arg == "-isysroot") {
1179         ++i;
1180         continue;
1181       }
1182       if (arg.find("-isysroot=") == 0 || (arg.find("-isysroot") == 0 && arg.length() > 9))
1183         continue;
1184 
1185       // Collect -isystem paths instead of passing them through
1186       // We'll add them at the end in the correct order (clang builtins first)
1187       if (arg == "-isystem" && i + 1 < args.size()) {
1188         collectedIsystemPaths.push_back(args[++i]);
1189         continue;
1190       }
1191       if (arg.find("-isystem") == 0 && arg.length() > 8) {
1192         collectedIsystemPaths.push_back(arg.substr(8));
1193         continue;
1194       }
1195 
1196       // Convert -I to -iquote for all project include paths
1197       // Convert -I to -isystem for dependency paths (so they come after our clang builtins)
1198       // This prevents <stdio.h> and <stdbool.h> from being searched in project directories
1199       // before system include paths.
1200       //
1201       // LibTooling's cc1 mode searches -I paths BEFORE -isystem paths for <stdio.h>, which
1202       // causes it to look in the project's include directory before system headers. By converting
1203       // all project -I to -iquote, <stdio.h> will skip project paths and find the system header.
1204       //
1205       // For public API headers like <ascii-chat/header.h>, using -iquote works fine because:
1206       // -iquote paths come first in the search order for quoted includes, and quoted includes
1207       // can find both public and private headers.
1208       if (arg == "-I" && i + 1 < args.size()) {
1209         // -I /path/to/dir (separate argument)
1210         const std::string &includePath = args[++i];
1211         if (isProjectPath(includePath)) {
1212           // Keep public include/ visible for angle includes (<ascii-chat/...>).
1213           // Other project paths remain quote-only to avoid shadowing system headers.
1214           if (isPublicIncludePath(includePath)) {
1215             collectedIsystemPaths.push_back(includePath);
1216           } else {
1217             result.push_back("-iquote");
1218             result.push_back(includePath);
1219           }
1220         } else {
1221           // Collect dependency -I paths to add as -isystem after our builtins
1222           collectedIsystemPaths.push_back(includePath);
1223         }
1224         continue;
1225       }
1226       if (arg.find("-I") == 0 && arg.length() > 2) {
1227         // -I/path/to/dir (combined form)
1228         std::string includePath = arg.substr(2);
1229         if (isProjectPath(includePath)) {
1230           // Keep public include/ visible for angle includes (<ascii-chat/...>).
1231           // Other project paths remain quote-only to avoid shadowing system headers.
1232           if (isPublicIncludePath(includePath)) {
1233             collectedIsystemPaths.push_back(includePath);
1234           } else {
1235             result.push_back("-iquote");
1236             result.push_back(includePath);
1237           }
1238         } else {
1239           // Collect dependency -I paths to add as -isystem after our builtins
1240           collectedIsystemPaths.push_back(includePath);
1241         }
1242         continue;
1243       }
1244 
1245       result.push_back(arg);
1246     }
1247 
1248     // Fourth: add system include paths in the correct order
1249     // Order matters for LibTooling on LLVM 21 - clang builtins MUST come first
1250     // to shadow any phantom VFS entries that might be created for other paths
1251     for (const auto &arg : appendArgs) {
1252       result.push_back(arg);
1253     }
1254     // Then add the collected -isystem paths from the compilation database
1255     for (const auto &path : collectedIsystemPaths) {
1256       result.push_back("-isystem");
1257       result.push_back(path);
1258     }
1259 
1260     // Fifth: add the defer tool define and separator
1261     result.push_back("-DASCIICHAT_DEFER_TOOL_PARSING");
1262     if (foundSeparator) {
1263       result.push_back("--");
1264       // Copy any remaining args after "--"
1265       for (size_t i = separatorIndex + 1; i < args.size(); ++i) {
1266         result.push_back(args[i]);
1267       }
1268     }
1269 
1270     return result;
1271   };
1272   tool.appendArgumentsAdjuster(consolidatedAdjuster);
1273 
1274   DeferActionFactory actionFactory(outputDir, inputRoot);
1275   const int executionResult = tool.run(&actionFactory);
1276   if (executionResult != 0) {
1277     llvm::errs() << "Defer transformation failed with code " << executionResult << "\n";
1278   }
1279   return executionResult;
1280 }

  std::string > & outputRegistry ()

  path)

46   auto &registry = outputRegistry();
47   bool inserted = registry.insert(path).second;
48   return inserted;

  unregisterOutputPath (const std::string & path)
